treatment_information.treatment_response: The neuropathic pain syndrome was well controlled with Pregabalin and a low-dose antidepressant.
The neuropathic pain syndrome was well controlled with Pregabalin and a low-dose antidepressant.

treatment_timeline.event: Patient experienced chronic facial pain and left ptosis for about 10 years.
Patient underwent multiple MRI examinations without finding a morphological correlate.
Patient tried various neurological therapy attempts under different suspected diagnoses (e.g., trigeminal neuralgia, migraine, cluster headache) without success.
The pain suddenly disappeared, leaving only slight left-sided facial hypesthesia.
A new MRI showed a subacute hemorrhage into a small brainstem cavernoma.
Neurosurgical evaluation deemed the operation of the superficially located cavernoma possible but too risky due to the well-documented benign course over 10 years.
Patient developed a new massive neuropathic pain syndrome.
Patient remained symptom-free under reduced medication.

patient_outcome.recovery_timeline: Patient remained symptom-free about 6 months after treatment.
